records.ety: [OE. feture form, shape, feature, OF. faiture fashion, make, fr. L. factura a making, formation, fr. facere, factum, to make. See Feat, Fact, and cf. Facture.]

defs: The make, form, or outward appearance of a person; the whole turn or style of the body; esp., good appearance.
quotes: What needeth it his feature to descrive?
Cheated of feature by dissembling nature.

defs: The make, cast, or appearance of the human face, and especially of any single part of the face; a lineament. (pl.) The face, the countenance.
quotes: It is for homely features to keep home.

defs: The cast or structure of anything, or of any part of a thing, as of a landscape, a picture, a treaty, or an essay; any marked peculiarity or characteristic; as, one of the features of the landscape.
quotes: And to her service bind each living creature Through secret understanding of their feature.
So scented the grim feature, and upturned His nostril wide into the murky air.
